hints.text: Plates white EU **or** UK-style **yellow rear**, mostly w/ EU strip; **red rental plates** common
Drives on the **left** (ex-UK colony — UK-style signs & road lines too)

shared_with.note: Malta also drives left with UK heritage.
shared_with.diff: Maltese/English + globigerina limestone → Malta; Greek script → Cyprus.

shared_with.note: The infrastructure itself is borrowed from the UK.

shared_with.diff: Cyprus drives LEFT + English co-signage; Greece drives right.

hints.text: Mostly Gen4 **smallcam** (low mount, big blur, front protrusion) — sometimes a **short antenna** visible at the back

shared_with.note: Greece has much smallcam too — but **never a visible antenna**.
shared_with.diff: Antenna → Cyprus. Driving side decides anyway.

hints.text: Bollards like Turkish ones (red front / white back reflector) but **triangular with a slanted top**, not flat

shared_with.diff: Turkish ones are **flat**; Cypriot triangular + slanted.

hints.text: Directional signs ≈ Greek ones — but Greek text is NOT all-caps (Greece: all-caps); **town-entry signs** (yellow w/ red border or white, bilingual + speed limit above) are unique to Cyprus
Tall **dark-brown wooden poles** (like Greece); poletops: crossbar w/ 0/3 insulators, 5 side insulators, or harp-shaped frame — the **symmetric 3-insulator top is Cyprus-specific**

shared_with.diff: The symmetric 3-insulator top → Cyprus; driving side otherwise.

hints.text: Brightly painted **stone buildings**, tiled or flat roofs (Greek-like); houses often carry a **white horizontal cylindrical water tank** w/ circular lid
Mediterranean: bright exposed rock, olive/palm/cypress trees, green coastal farmland, pine-forest mountains inland

Only the **southern half** covered (Republic + Akrotiri & Dhekelia); road numbers A/B/E/F grouped by first digit (E/F near matching B road); 5 provinces = 2-digit area codes (2x); postal codes 4-digit on street signs (T.K./T.T.)
**Terrain = compass**: centre = highest; white limestone → south coast; reddish soil → interior + NW; vineyards → SW; wild mustard (yellow flowers) → mostly east; dark red fields → north + Famagusta
**Paphos Forest**: windy mountain roads, red soil, uninhabited — and (almost) no bollards; **NE**: Kyrenia ridge visible north over flat farmland; NE also has **black-top bollards** (Slovenia-like)
**Famagusta** (far east): flat + red fields — and ALL coverage is **shitcam** (rare elsewhere on the island)
**Wind turbines** in 2 clusters: west of Larnaca (sandy rolling farm hills) + SW of Anogyra (Oreites Forest, incline toward the visible coast)

**Spotlights**: snow coverage near Panagia Machaira monastery; dashed **blue lines** between road markings around Mount Olympus; banana plantations around Peyia (west coast); **Limassol** = Russian on many commercial signs (17% Russian-speaking)
